response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack software engineer with roughly 15+ years of experience spanning web, mobile, and some low-latency backend systems, which satisfies the formal education-plus-experience bar. However, the role demands deep hardware-oriented expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The closest relevant experience (low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments) is far removed from silicon-level performance architecture and deep-learning workload optimization.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years qualification path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical community leadership
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance-analysis experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not principal-level hardware or systems architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload experience
